fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Vermont Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Vermont - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity vermont, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to vermont, pharmacy technician compact vermont, vermont pharmacy technician endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Vermont.

steps.title: Submit an Application
steps.description: Complete the Pharmacy Technician Registration Application and submit it to the Vermont Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pay the Required Fee
steps.description: Pay the $100 registration fee to the Vermont Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Continuing Education
steps.description: Complete 20 hours of continuing education every two years to maintain your registration.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Vermont pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Vermont have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Vermont. To work in Vermont, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Vermont?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Vermont while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Vermont
hero.dek: Vermont Pharmacy Technician registration requires submitting an application, paying a fee, and completing continuing education.
